source_pr.title: *: stabilize some tests for CI
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

stabilize some tests for CI, Fix #12685, Fix #12942

### What is changed and how it works?

Enlarge timeout

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

- Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

    N/A

symptoms: Tests intermittently fail in CI due to too-short timeouts / insufficient waiting for async components (schema syncer / schema validator) to become ready.
failure_signature: Timeout / readiness check failure in `ddl/util/syncer_test.go` (100ms context timeout) or `domain/domain_test.go` (bounded wait for `SchemaValidator.IsStarted()`), especially under slow CI.
root_cause_explanation: The tests were flaky due to async timing variability in CI: the test used very short timeouts (e.g. 100ms) and bounded polling loops when waiting for schema-sync related state to become ready. Under slow/loaded CI this can exceed the timeout and fail intermittently. The PR stabilizes it by increasing the timeouts (e.g. 100ms → 1s / 20ms → 200ms) and waiting for the schema validator/syncer to start before asserting.
fix_pattern: Increase timeouts and wait for async components to be ready: replace too-short context timeouts / bounded polling with longer timeouts or wait-until-started logic.

evidence: evidence: *: stabilize some tests for CI
